latestDirtyPackageRefreshStatus: source-feedback Task 3 residual selected-spell route-task queue, Task 8 six-driver active reducer diagnostic seed, FCSF-04 object stale public route-history evidence, FCSF-05 reaction/interrupt payload taxonomy evidence, FCSF-06 character/sheet/handoff rejection and resource payload evidence, and SQNT-07B/C/D feature-species-metamagic route evidence are packaged in cleanroom-input. FCSF-04 dirty replay later accepted 6 connector rows and covered 7 object-route obligations, FCSF-05 dirty replay accepted all 5 connector rows, FCSF-06 dirty replay accepted 30 reducer-route witness rows and recorded 14 target blockers, SQNT-07B dirty replay accepted 15 selected species/passive-adjacent rows with 2 inherited Adrenaline Rush rows out of scope, SQNT-07C dirty replay accepted 30 selected metamagic rows, and SQNT-07D dirty replay accepted 3 active-feature spell-benefit rows; no fresh target acceptance is claimed from these dirty replays.

laneSizingPolicy.reason: Fresh expansion lanes should stay smaller than dirty rehearsal lanes because each accepted row must also strengthen verifier coverage and cleanroom-boundary evidence.

lanes.purpose: Lock the accepted fresh target baseline and create expansion-state bookkeeping before implementation lanes.
Convert FC-01 diagnostic reducer substrate support into focused current-package route evidence for small battle lifecycle drivers.
expectedOutputs: STATE_OWNERS.md additions if new durable owners are introduced
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Observed route events for slot-spell, HP restoration, Death Saving Throw, and Concentration teardown come from reducer entrypoints. The verifier regenerates expected records from connector action bodies and observed records from a Rust public-entrypoint example.

lanes.purpose: Revisit the two FC-03 blocked selected spell groups only through generic spell-attack and save-gated connector substrates.
expectedOutputs: BLOCKERS.json updates for any still-uncovered branches
verifier update for unblocked branch subset
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Accepted with blockers: generic spell-attack and save-gated substrates route through shared reducer entrypoints; residual selected spell effects outside that substrate remain blocked, and production code does not branch on spell identity.

lanes.purpose: Add generic chained spell-attack and object-target spell-attack procedure routes that support later selected damage spell groups.
expectedOutputs: spell attack procedure owner state-owner records
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Historical fresh snapshot accepted with blockers: chained and independent spell attack fills route through generic SpellAttackProcedure owners without collapsing them into one attack replay island; isolated object stale replay was blocked in this snapshot. FCSF-04 later resolves that blocker as source input by packaging public object stale route-history evidence; dirty replay accepts 6 connector rows and covers all 7 Starry Wisp object obligations. Fresh target replay is still pending.

lanes.purpose: Expand active-effect lifecycle, scalar buff, roll modifier, and turn-boundary cleanup substrates before selected active-effect drivers depend on them.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Accepted: generic roll-modifier active-effect, scalar-buff active-effect, targeted-speed scalar buff, turn-boundary cleanup, exact skill/ability/two-target ability route-fill payload surfaces, Ability Check/Search public reducer surfaces, failed-save/voluntary-end/replacement Concentration cleanup, and scalar-buff profile projection/domain sequencing route through public reducer entrypoints. Exact roll-choice payloads were resolved source-side by commit 0c2ba34c5a45f18b73dfe590e0e86419ba377375 and accepted in the fresh target by b43797af240c1486e5ad92c3698bf2cd2958a91e. Ability Check/Search public observability was accepted by cd4465556d18729121f56f5834ac00f8b0b3d15c. Concentration cleanup source input landed at c62aa73be7f80e4d3a5b460aa2bef42cea0c0f7d and was accepted by bd6c6ba2407ac00a8295bbe1cd66a70e5ae8364c. Scalar profile source input landed at ee4894fa71e9307b9251639f0b54577ff764c63f and was accepted by 05280a8e2d6e9705411c114c80ae2a4e4290de2c.

lanes.purpose: Add reaction casting-time and interrupt-stack resume routing before broader reaction spell selected-identity branches are attempted.

lanes.acceptanceSummary: Historical fresh snapshot accepted with blockers: generic reaction casting-time and interrupt-stack resume route surfaces route through public reducer entrypoints; selected reaction spell projections and interrupt trigger taxonomy were blocked in this snapshot. FCSF-05 later resolves those blockers as source input by packaging generic reaction/interrupt payload taxonomy evidence; dirty replay accepts all 5 connector rows and covers 3 selected-context obligations. Fresh target replay is still pending.

lanes.purpose: Grow character creation, sheet, and handoff surfaces beyond the SDK tracer while preserving character-side ownership and battle handoff boundaries.
expectedOutputs: SDK tracer extension if public surface changes
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Historical fresh snapshot accepted with blockers: character creation finalization, sheet hit-point route projection, short-rest Pact-slot completion, and happy-path battle settlement surfaces route through public character/sheet/handoff entrypoints; expected records are mechanically derived from copied QNT connector action bodies/helper vocabulary. FCSF-06 later resolves the partial/rejection/resource/conflict source-input blockers by packaging typed character/sheet/handoff route facts. The dirty cleanroom replay accepted 30 rows and recorded 14 target blockers; fresh target replay is still pending.

lanes.purpose: Probe high-risk feature/species/metamagic selected drivers only through generic substrate connectors.

lanes.acceptanceSummary: Accepted with blockers in the historical fresh target: feature bonus-action Dash/temporary-hit-point, species creature-stat projection, metamagic resource governor, and active-feature Spell Save DC benefit route surfaces pass through public reducer entrypoints with connector-derived expected records. Current-package dirty replay now also accepts SQNT-07B/C/D rows through copied route connectors and observed reducer entrypoints. Selected/grouped identity witnesses and residual species/metamagic/feature branches remain explicit source-input work before fresh target replay can claim them.
